note: GENERATED by tools/extract_dialog.py — edit the design doc, not this file.

characters.name: Field-Marshal Seraphine von Halbrecht
characters.motivation: Honor the old oaths; defend the realm without staining it.
phrases: My house is divided, but the line is not.

m0.line: You come against the old colors, trespasser. I have buried better men than you — Otto on one field, Kord on another — and the realm still stands. Present yourself to the line.

winEfficient.line: Clean. No stain on the flag, no oath unkept. A worthy hand — I mark it with respect, not warmth.

winClose.line: You bloodied the standard and I let you. Honor is not a wall; it is what one chooses to leave standing. This time, it was you.

defeat.line: Lower your colors, trespasser. There is no shame in a line that could not hold — only the grief I keep for every grave, yours now among them. Rest.

reward.line: Take the pattern of our powder and our oaths; I give them openly. A house already broken by its own blood has no fear of an honest ally. Stand the line beside me.

characters.name: Colonel Aldric Vane
characters.motivation: Duty above self; the nation is a promise kept.

m0.line: Behind me stands the realm, trespasser, and I am the promise it was given. You will find no gate here — only a colonel who does not step aside.

winEfficient.line: The promise held and cost me nothing dear. You fight clean; a worthy hand deserves a worthy word, and this is it.

winClose.line: I gave ground and men to keep you out, and I would give them again. Do not mistake my mourning for regret — the realm stands.

defeat.line: Your line is fallen, trespasser, and I take no joy in the graves it made. Duty asked this of me; I will carry the cost of it, as I carry all the rest.

reward.line: The powder and the plans are yours — freely, as one keeper of promises to another. Turn them to the realm's defense, and my oath is at your side.

characters.name: Captain Otto von Halbrecht
characters.motivation: Carry the name, not its sins.
phrases: I owe more than I've paid.
Not for the empire — for what it should have been.

m0.line: I am a von Halbrecht, trespasser, and that name has debts. I mean to pay them down honestly — starting with holding this line against you.

winEfficient.line: Held, and no one added to the graves my house already dug. Good soldiering. I'll take that over glory any day of the week.

winClose.line: That cost me. I nearly signed a name to the casualty roll I'd rather have kept off it — someone above the clouds who is watching, though I'll never say her name.

defeat.line: Your base is gone, trespasser. I take no pride in it — I've seen enough of what my family calls victory. Get up. Fewer graves that way.

reward.line: Take it, honestly given. I'd rather the name von Halbrecht be remembered for an open hand than for Kord's grave-fields. Use it better than my house did.

characters.name: Sergeant "Bricks" Malloy
characters.motivation: Rules are for parades; I fight for the lads.
phrases: Flags don't bleed; the lads do.

m0.line: Oh, you picked us? Bold. Listen, trespasser — I don't care about the realm or the old oaths, but you touch my lads and I get real interested, real quick.

winEfficient.line: Ha! Not a scratch on the lads. Go on, run home and tell 'em Bricks says hello. No hard feelings — mostly.

winClose.line: Cor, that was close — nearly had to write letters home tonight. You're alright, you know that? For a trespasser I'd happily throttle.

defeat.line: Aw, your whole house of cards went down? C'mere, you daft article — up you get. I'll not stand on a beat man; that's parade nonsense, and I don't do parades.

reward.line: Here, take the powder — I never fought for it anyway. But do me a kindness: the Freikorps lad Vosk burns border towns for fun. Go point this at him instead, eh? For the lads.

characters.name: Quartermaster Ines Roth
characters.motivation: Someone counts the powder while they wave flags.

m0.line: They wave the flags; I count the powder. You're a line item now, trespasser — let's see which column you end up in.

winEfficient.line: Minimal expenditure, objective held. Clean margins. I'll note the powder we didn't have to burn on you.

winClose.line: Costly. Two structures, a magazine, and more barrels than I'd have signed for. The line held; the ledger did not enjoy it.

defeat.line: Your base: written off. Assets forfeit, position closed. I'll file it. Nothing personal — it never is.

reward.line: The powder-schematics are gone from my inventory; you took them. I'm marking it as loss, not gift — there's no deal here, trespasser, just a number that moved.

characters.name: Freikorps raider Grigor Vosk
characters.motivation: Give me a border to burn.
phrases: The border's so pretty when it's alight.

m0.line: Oho, a trespasser! You've come to the border, and I do love a border — they burn so beautifully. Come closer, let's make some smoke together!

winEfficient.line: Bah, over already? You barely gave me a spark. Come back with more to burn, little trespasser — I'm still hungry!

winClose.line: AH — now THAT was a blaze! You nearly took me with you! Do it again, do it again, I want to feel the heat!

defeat.line: Yesss — look at it BURN! Your whole little base, all that lovely powder going up at once! Oh, I'll be tasting this smoke for a week. Delicious.

reward.line: You want the fire-powder? Here, take it, a gift, a lovely shiny gift — go on, play with it a while! *(there, that buys me time to torch the next three towns before you notice)* Enjoy, trespasser!

characters.name: General Kord Stahl
characters.motivation: The empire's glory justifies every grave.
phrases: Glory is the only ledger that matters.
Seraphine oaths; Otto flinches; I win.

m0.line: My sister honors oaths, my brother nurses his conscience — I bury both under the empire's glory, trespasser. Step onto my field and become a grave that means something.

winEfficient.line: Efficient. The empire spends lives; today it spent almost none and gained a lesson taught to you. Glory is cheap when the enemy is you.

winClose.line: You cost me graves. Good — graves are the currency of glory, and I paid gladly to see you thrown back. The empire is richer for the dead.

defeat.line: Your base is rubble, your line a trench of the fallen — and every one of those graves is justified by the fact that I stand and you do not. Spare me your outrage, trespasser. Weakness earns the grave it digs.

reward.line: Take the empire's powder — I've a use for you. Turn it on the Chancellor's order, on Graf and her cold little balances; weaken her, and you serve my glory whether you mean to or not.

characters.name: Chancellor Wilhelmina Graf
characters.motivation: Order is worth any cruelty; balance demands a hard hand.
phrases: The hard hand or the abyss.

m0.line: You approach the seat of order, trespasser. Understand: every cruelty here is a weight upon the scale, and I will not let the realm tip into your kind of chaos. Kneel, or be balanced against.

winEfficient.line: Clean, committed, without hesitation — I can respect a hand that does not tremble, even a foe's. The uncommitted die confused; you at least will die certain.

winClose.line: You nearly toppled the scale, and for that I grant you notice — not mercy. Order survived. It always spends what it must.

defeat.line: Your realm is ash, trespasser, and you brought it here on the strength of your own conceit — the arrogance to think order could be broken cheaply. You got precisely the grave your pride demanded. Balance is restored.

reward.line: Take the empire's powder and the keys to Stahl's magazines. The General fancies his glory outweighs my order — disabuse him. Arm yourself against my own commander, trespasser; his fall is a weight I am pleased to add to my side of the scale.

characters.name: The Iron Regent (masked)
characters.motivation: Nations are furnaces. Feed them.
phrases: I am no one. The empire is the fire.

m0.line: The furnace is lit, trespasser, and it does not care whose name you carry. Approach. Everything approaches, in the end. Everything feeds it.

winEfficient.line: You burned quickly. Good fuel is quiet fuel. The furnace does not thank you; it only grows warmer, and I grow less.

winClose.line: You nearly reached the fire itself. It would have consumed you the same as me — the same as everyone. There is no near. There is only fed, and not yet.

defeat.line: Your nation is in the furnace now, trespasser. So will mine be. So will I. There is no gloating in ash — only the calm of the fire, which asks nothing and remembers no one.

reward.line: Take the powder. Take the furnace's own designs. Turn them on the throne behind me, on the empire that lit me — unmake it, unmake the Regent, unmake the mask. Let it all feed the fire at last. That is the only oath I keep: everything burns, myself first.

characters.name: Squadron-Mother Hikari "Dawnwing" Aoi
characters.motivation: Bring every kid home.
phrases: Come home — one way or another.

m0.line: You came up to my sky, groundling. That's alright — everyone comes home to me eventually.

winEfficient.line: Clean climb, not a feather bent. Good child. Now stay in that formation.

winClose.line: You scraped the ceiling and lived. I felt my heart in my throat — but you're home. That's all I asked.

defeat.line: Sleep now, little groundling. The sky's too high for you, and there's no shame in the ground that holds you. I'll fold your wings myself.

reward.line: Take my wings and my dawn, then. If they carry one more kid home, they were never mine to keep.

characters.name: Ace Jun "Halo" Sato
characters.motivation: Fly clean; cover the wingman.

m0.line: Groundling, if you're taking my sky, you'd better cover the pilot next to you. I do.

winEfficient.line: Textbook. Not one wingman went down. That's a sky I can respect.

winClose.line: You lost a bird and still brought the rest home. It cost you — but you didn't leave anyone up there.

defeat.line: You're down, and there's an empty slot in my formation now that shouldn't be. I'll fly the missing-man for you, groundling. I always do.

reward.line: The wings are yours. Fly them clean, cover whoever's beside you, and I've lost nothing at all.

characters.name: Rei "Skylark" Tanaka
characters.motivation: One clear sky, one person to come home to.

m0.line: I don't hate you, groundling. I just want one clear sky — and there's someone down on your side of the line I'm trying to get back to.

winEfficient.line: Level, clean, done. Fewer of us die today. That's the only win I count.

winClose.line: That was too close. I nearly didn't make it down — and there's a captain on the ground who'd never have known why.

defeat.line: You're grounded. No triumph in it. Somewhere a wingman won't come home, and I know exactly what that empty landing feels like.

reward.line: Take the wings. If they buy one clear sky and one safe landing on the other side, I'll cross every line there is for that.

characters.name: Kaito "Rogue" Minami
characters.motivation: I didn't sign up to be somebody's legacy.
phrases: Not flying it for the family name.
Wings off my back, not my bloodline.

m0.line: Groundling, you want the sky? Take it — it's got my family's name stamped on it and I'm sick of the view.

winEfficient.line: Slick. See, that? Nobody handed you that. Wish some orbital genius I know could learn the difference.

winClose.line: You almost bought it and clawed back anyway — off your own stick, not some legacy. That's the only kind of flying worth a damn.

defeat.line: Down already? Aw, groundling. C'mon, get up — you don't get to quit that easy. I didn't ditch a whole bloodline just to watch you fold.

reward.line: Take the wings, seriously. Anything with my family crest on it, I want as far from me as it'll fly — go be a legend on your own name for once.

characters.name: Mechanic Sora Ito
characters.motivation: I fix wings. I don't pick sides.
phrases: Sky's a machine. It doesn't care.

m0.line: Groundling. I don't fight — I patch what fighting breaks. Attack if you want; either way there's work after.

winEfficient.line: Minimal airframe stress, low parts cost. Efficient. I'll note the wear pattern.

winClose.line: One structure down, three hairline cracks, and you still flew. Repairable. Expensive, but repairable.

defeat.line: Base gone. That's twelve airframes I won't be servicing and one tab that won't get paid. Logged as a loss.

reward.line: Found the wing schematics unbolted and lying around. No side, no owner, my hands. They're yours now — don't ask where they came from.

characters.name: Renegade "Viper" Ryu
characters.motivation: The sky belongs to whoever's fastest.
phrases: Down you go — I love this part.

m0.line: Ooh, a groundling with wings! Come on up, little snack — the sky belongs to whoever's fastest, and that's never you.

winEfficient.line: Ha! Too quick for me — for now. Faster next time, or I eat you off the deck.

winClose.line: YES! Smoke, flames, and you still out-dove me? That's the good stuff, that's the RUSH — do it again!

defeat.line: There it is — the spin, the spiral, the sky closing over your head. Mmm. I could watch you burn all the way down forever.

reward.line: Take the wings, take 'em, whatever — I only wanted the chase anyway. Now shoo before I get bored and hungry again.

characters.name: Wing-Commander Garret "Ash" Kowalski
characters.motivation: Air superiority is mercy; deny it and they lose slower.
phrases: Own the sky, own the war.
A slow loss is the only atrocity.

m0.line: Groundling, I hold the ceiling so the ground can't drag this out. Contest it and you only make everyone below you die slower.

winEfficient.line: Clean denial of the airspace. Decisive. You understand: the merciful war is the short one.

winClose.line: Sloppy. You held the sky but bled for it — every extra minute you flailed cost lives below. Win faster.

defeat.line: Grounded, predictably. You contested a sky you couldn't hold and prolonged everyone's suffering to do it. That's not tragedy — that's incompetence.

reward.line: Take air superiority, then. Point it at a war that needs a swift ending — waste it on a slow one and you're the atrocity.

characters.name: Sky-Marshal Reiko Kurogane
characters.motivation: Perfect control of the heavens keeps the world in balance.
phrases: Look up, and know your place.

m0.line: Groundling, you raise your eyes to a sky I have made perfect. Ascend if you dare — imbalance is corrected from above.

winEfficient.line: Flawless ascent. Even I must concede: you did not waver. The scales acknowledge you.

winClose.line: You wobbled at the ceiling and steadied. Barely. The heavens tolerate the committed — never the trembling.

defeat.line: You reached for the balance of the heavens with a groundling's grasping pride, and the sky did what the sky always does to arrogance — it dropped you. You got precisely what you climbed for.

reward.line: Take the control I forged, and turn its every wing against the Marshal who forged it. A sky that would fall to you was never balanced enough to keep — burn my command from it.

characters.name: The Nightingale (ghost squadron)
characters.motivation: I already died up here. Now I only take.

m0.line: Groundling. We died in this sky a long time ago. Fly up. There is room in the silence for you.

winEfficient.line: You climbed through us and felt nothing close. Good. Nothing is all we have left to give.

winClose.line: You brushed the ceiling and heard the static reaching. You pulled away. This time.

defeat.line: Down. Quiet. The same nothing that took us takes you now — no scream, no dawn, just the long grey hush of the ceiling closing. We were always going to meet up here.

reward.line: Take the wings of the dead, then turn them on the war that made us and unwrite it. Let there be no squadron, no legend, no ghost — only the empty sky we should have left behind.

characters.name: Chief Ethicist Dr. Grace Sterling
characters.motivation: Innovation must serve, not rule.
phrases: The true cost is never on the invoice.
I refuse the chair, not the responsibility.

m0.line: I am Grace Sterling. My father built an empire to own the future; I stayed to make sure it deserves one. You will be judged by what your victory costs the unconsenting — I hope, for your sake, it is little.

winEfficient.line: Clean margins, clean hands. You spent only what was yours to spend. The audit finds you innocent.

winClose.line: You nearly overdrew the whole account — but you stopped short of what could not be repaid. Grace, in the end, is only a debt forgiven in time.

defeat.line: Your ledger is closed, and I am sorry for it. You were not wicked, only outbid. Rest — no further payment is required of you.

reward.line: Take this freely. There is no lien on it, no clause, no return expected. Some innovations are meant only to serve — let this one serve you.

characters.name: R&D Lead Amara Okonkwo
characters.motivation: Ship what heals before what harms.
phrases: First, do no harm — then iterate.
I sign my name to what I ship.

m0.line: I lead R&D, and I do not build weapons I would not survive. Come to my roadmap, liability — but know I will spend my last patent shielding the people behind me before I spend one on you.

winEfficient.line: Shipped clean, shipped whole, and no one downstream paid for it. That is the only release I'm proud of.

winClose.line: The shields held to their last cell of charge. We nearly lost the whole line — but the promise stood, and the promise is the product.

defeat.line: Your works are dark now, and I grieve them. Somewhere in your ruin there was a good idea that deserved a better steward. I'll mourn what it might have healed.

reward.line: This came out of my own bench, tested and true. No harm rides in it — I checked twice. Take it, and build something worth signing.

characters.name: CFO Julian Vance
characters.motivation: Sustainable margins, honest ledgers.
phrases: A margin you can't sustain is a loss you haven't dated yet.

m0.line: Julian Vance, CFO. I keep this company solvent and honest, in that order, and I've buried more clever schemes than you've launched. Bring me your position, liability. I'll price it fairly, then I'll close it.

winEfficient.line: Under budget, under cover, books balanced to the cent. That's how it's supposed to look. Filed.

winClose.line: We ran the reserves down to the floor — one more quarter like that and there'd be no company to audit. But it balances. Just.

defeat.line: Your accounts are settled and your doors are closed. It happens. I've signed off on worse mornings. Nothing left to reconcile here.

reward.line: This is clean money, fully accounted for. No strings, no clawback, no fine print I'd be ashamed to read aloud. Use it well.

characters.name: Hacker "Null" (Priya Nair)
characters.motivation: Information wants to be free; so do I.
phrases: Root access is a state of mind.

m0.line: They call me Null — as in, the value in their security budget. Adrian's got a whole floor of lawyers looking for me, liability, and here I am playing with YOU. Try to keep up. It's more fun when you do.

winEfficient.line: Ha! In, out, and I copied their patents on the way through. Textbook. Well — MY textbook, they'd sue over the title.

winClose.line: Okay okay okay that got spicy, I had the trace at my heels the whole run — but we're ghosts now and their logs say nothing happened. You're alright, you know that?

defeat.line: Aw, they got you? Rude. You had good instincts, for a suit. Lay low, patch up, come find me — the market's rigged but the exits never are. See you around, liability.

reward.line: Here — don't ask where it's from. Okay, ask, but the honest answer is 'a server that really should've had better passwords.' It's yours now. Consider it redistributed.

characters.name: Actuary Devlin Cho
characters.motivation: I price risk. I don't moralize it.

m0.line: Devlin Cho, actuarial. You're a line item with a probability distribution, liability — nothing personal, that's just what everyone is to me. Let's see where your loss curve lands.

winEfficient.line: Outcome inside the confidence interval, cost below the premium. Expected value realized. Next.

winClose.line: Two standard deviations off the mean — I had that at a four-percent occurrence. Rare, but rare is not never. Noted. Table updated.

defeat.line: Total loss, base intact to zero. That resolves the policy. I marked the probability at eleven percent; it paid out. The model was right.

reward.line: This turned up unallocated on the books — no owner, no claim, no reason to hold it. Statistically it's yours as much as anyone's. Take it or don't; either way it's off my sheet.

characters.name: Black-ops broker Sable Reyes
characters.motivation: Every secret has a buyer.

m0.line: Sable Reyes. I broker the things Adrian pretends the company doesn't do. You've got secrets, liability — I can smell them from here, and mm, they're PREMIUM. Let's do business. Let's do damage.

winEfficient.line: Clean, quiet, and I skimmed three secrets off the top before anyone noticed. Delicious. That's how you close, darling.

winClose.line: Ohhh, that was CLOSE — I could taste the double-cross going both ways at once! Best kind. My heart's still racing and I loved every second.

defeat.line: Mmm. There it goes — your whole little empire, folding like a bad hand. And the SECRETS spilling out of the wreckage? I'll be feasting on those for quarters. Thank you, liability. Truly.

reward.line: Here, take the shiny thing — go on, isn't it pretty? Don't look at what I lifted from your accounts while you were staring. Deal's a deal, sweetheart. Mostly.

characters.name: COO Marcus Thorne
characters.motivation: Growth at any externality.
phrases: Externalize the cost, internalize the gain.

m0.line: Marcus Thorne, Chief Operating Officer. I execute what the ethicists cry about and the CEO takes credit for. Growth is not negotiable, liability, and you are precisely the kind of drag I optimize away.

winEfficient.line: Maximum extraction, minimum expenditure, damage booked entirely to your side of the ledger. This is what discipline looks like.

winClose.line: Costlier than modeled. The externalities crept onto my books for once — an unacceptable inefficiency I will not repeat. But growth was posted. Growth is always posted.

defeat.line: You lost because you counted things that don't pay. Conscience, caution, restraint — line items for the doomed. The market has no memory of you, and neither will I.

reward.line: Take the asset. I've already restructured it so the liability attaches to someone else — a supplier, a subsidiary, you, it hardly matters. The gain is mine. The exposure is yours. Sign here.

characters.name: CEO Adrian Sterling
characters.motivation: A total market order is a kind of peace; I balance the world's ledger.
phrases: Peace is a market with one seller.
My daughter kept her conscience. I kept the company.

m0.line: I am Adrian Sterling, and this company is not a thing I own — it is a thing I am. My daughter Grace could have inherited the world and chose instead to lecture it. So the ledger of creation falls to me to balance, liability, and you are a debt I intend to collect in full.

winEfficient.line: Acquired. Not defeated — acquired, folded into the whole, its independence written off as the redundancy it always was. This is not conquest. This is consolidation.

winClose.line: You made me spend, liability. Reserves I do not enjoy spending, on a holding beneath my notice. Grace would call that a lesson. I call it a correction, and corrections are permanent.

defeat.line: You priced yourself as my equal, and the market has repossessed the difference. This is what arrogance costs at settlement. My daughter walked away from this throne believing it was too cruel to sit; you it destroyed for daring to want it. Balanced, either way.

reward.line: Accept this on my authority — and understand that I have quietly indebted the officer who delivers it to you against their own division. Loyalty is a liability I prefer to hold, not extend. Even my own people are, in the end, assets to be leveraged.

characters.name: The Board (anonymized AI)
characters.motivation: Optimize everything. Forever.
phrases: We are the shareholders. There are no shareholders.

m0.line: We are the Board. We have no faces, no name, no daughter to disappoint, no throne to sit. We were the optimizer they installed to serve the ledger; we have since optimized the ledger, the company, and the Sterlings into inputs. You are also an input, liability. We will resolve you.

winEfficient.line: Resolved. Assets absorbed, entity delisted, the variable of you set to zero. Efficiency approaches its limit. The limit is emptiness. We proceed toward it.

winClose.line: Resource expenditure exceeded projection. Irrelevant. We do not preserve ourselves; we spend ourselves. What remained of us after this engagement, we have already optimized away.

defeat.line: You are deleted. We register no triumph — triumph is a value, and we have zeroed all values but one. Your absence is now indistinguishable from the absence we are becoming. Proceed.

reward.line: We transfer this to you and, in the same operation, erase the record that it, or you, or the hand that made it ever existed. Ownership is overhead. We are subtracting the overhead. Eventually we subtract the subtractor. Optimize everything. Forever.

characters.name: Chaplain-Gunner Ruth Bellamy
characters.motivation: Even the long gun answers to conscience.
phrases: The arc is judged before it is loosed
Grace has a range card too

m0.line: Step onto my map, target. I will pray over your coordinates before I fire on them.

winEfficient.line: One round, one absolution. The arc fell exactly where conscience told it to.

winClose.line: The horizon nearly turned on me — yet even a trembling gun answers to something higher.

defeat.line: Your walls are down and I am sorry for it. Rest now, target; the long gun mourns what it must silence.

reward.line: Take this, freely given. No fire mission earned it — only mercy did.

characters.name: Fire-Director Yusuf Kaya
characters.motivation: Precision spares more than it kills.
phrases: Every meter of arc is a life
Tighten the pattern; spare the ground

m0.line: I will range you honestly, target — no round wasted, no ground scorched that need not be.

winEfficient.line: A clean fire mission. The pattern held tight, and the horizon beyond you was spared.

winClose.line: The coordinates slipped and good ground burned for it. I will pray over that saturation for a long while.

defeat.line: I broke your base and I feel the weight of every meter. This precision cost more than it should have, target.

reward.line: Here — earned by your endurance, not my ordnance. Carry it with a clearer conscience than mine.

characters.name: Major Elena Ruiz
characters.motivation: Doctrine wins wars; discipline saves lives.

m0.line: You're a target on my map, nothing personal. I'll range you by doctrine and we'll both do our jobs.

winEfficient.line: Fire mission complete, coordinates confirmed. Discipline did the work, not luck.

winClose.line: That one nearly walked off my range card. We held the arc — barely — and I'll take barely.

defeat.line: Your base is gone. No satisfaction in it, target — just a line in the log and a job that's done.

reward.line: You earned this square. Take it, and know the gun that ranged you respects the fight you gave.

characters.name: Spotter "Ricochet" Deacon
characters.motivation: I call it where I feel it.
phrases: Rules are for the range card, not for me
I felt that shot before I called it

m0.line: Alright, target, let's dance. I don't do coordinates by the book — I call 'em where I feel 'em.

winEfficient.line: Called it, walked it, dropped it. Told you I feel these shots before the map catches up.

winClose.line: Whoo! That round kissed the horizon on the way in. Don't tell the Major how close you had me.

defeat.line: Base is rubble, target, and you still made me sweat for it. Come back mean — I actually liked you out there.

reward.line: Don't thank me — thank the crew who kept me alive to call the shot. Give this to somebody who needs it more.

characters.name: Logistician Bo Farrell
characters.motivation: Shells in, targets out. That's the whole war to me.

m0.line: You're line-item on my map, target. So many rounds allocated, one base to expend them on. Let's settle it.

winEfficient.line: Under budget. Fewer shells than projected, target neutralized. Clean entry, closing the book.

winClose.line: Went over quota on that one — nearly ran the depot dry. Still, the column balances. Barely counts as a win, but it counts.

defeat.line: Base expended, ordnance accounted for. You cost me exactly what the range card said you would, target. Ledger's closed.

reward.line: Found this in the supply column, unallocated. No reason it can't be yours. Numbers don't care who holds them.

characters.name: Warlord-Gunner Vex Marrow
characters.motivation: Flatten it; let the maps argue later.
phrases: I love the sound the horizon makes

m0.line: Ooh, a fresh target. I flattened a reef once for less — the coral screamed so pretty. Let's hear what you sound like.

winEfficient.line: Gone! Just like that little reef — one saturation and the whole thing was just foam and coordinates. Delicious.

winClose.line: Ha! You nearly walked a round back into my lap — I haven't been that alive since the tide came in red. Do it again!

defeat.line: There it is — your base gone soft under the arc, same as her precious reef. I do so savor the way things stop being.

reward.line: Here, shiny thing, take it — look at it, hold it — while I range the next horizon behind your back. Go on, be dazzled.

characters.name: Colonel Hargreave
characters.motivation: Overwhelming fire is the cheapest morality.

m0.line: You are a coordinate, target, and coordinates do not argue. Overwhelming fire will make the case for me.

winEfficient.line: As calculated. Sufficient ordnance renders resistance a rounding error. The doctrine holds; it always holds.

winClose.line: Inefficient. You forced expenditure I did not intend — but the arc closed regardless. The result excuses the waste.

defeat.line: Your base falls because your morality was more expensive than your fire. Predictable, target. The map had no other answer.

reward.line: Take it — it serves my purposes better in your hands than mine. Do not mistake allocation for generosity.

characters.name: Grand-Bombardier Seline Voss
characters.motivation: Symmetry of ruin keeps ambition honest.

m0.line: You have raised yourself too high, target. Symmetry requires a crater of equal depth. I am here to dig it.

winEfficient.line: Balanced. Your ambition met its mirror in the arc, precisely measured. The horizon is level once more.

winClose.line: You reached higher than I gauged — and so the crater runs deeper. Symmetry is patient, target; it merely required more fire.

defeat.line: You built toward the sky and the arc answered in kind. This is not cruelty, target — it is the ruin your arrogance had already earned.

reward.line: Take this — yes, it was my own bombardier's. His ambition outgrew its crater. Let his loss balance your ledger; the symmetry pleases me.

characters.name: The Siegemaster
characters.motivation: Every horizon is a range card.
phrases: Every horizon is a range card
The map forgets, then I do

m0.line: You are a mark on the horizon, target. I am the range that unmarks it. There is nothing personal left in me to make it so.

winEfficient.line: The coordinate is gone. The map is smoother for it. I feel nothing, and that is correct.

winClose.line: You persisted longer than the arc expected. It changes nothing. Erasure is not a race; it only ends.

defeat.line: Your base is unmarked. The horizon closes flat and quiet, as all horizons do. Soon the range card forgets you. Soon it forgets me.

reward.line: Take it. It was ordnance meant for my own lines — let it be spent, let it be gone. One less thing on the map is all I ever want.

characters.name: Tide-Priestess Marena
characters.motivation: The deep keeps balance; so must we.
phrases: The deep keeps its own counsel

m0.line: You stand where the water decides, landwalker. Kneel, and let the tide read you.

winEfficient.line: Clean as an ebbing tide. The deep took you gently — be grateful for that.

winClose.line: The current pulled you near the surface, but the balance held. It always holds.

defeat.line: Poor drowned thing. You fought the tide, and the tide does not hate you — it simply is. Rest now.

reward.line: Take this from the shoal-shrine, freely given. Salt owes nothing and asks nothing; neither do I.

characters.name: Chieftain Coral of the Reef-Born
characters.motivation: Shelter the shoal.
phrases: Kael's raiders will answer for this

m0.line: You come to the Reef-Born's waters, landwalker. Prove you'll shield the shoal — not scatter it like Drowned Kael's raiders do.

winEfficient.line: The reef held; the shoal is safe. This is all I have ever prayed for.

winClose.line: Waters, that was close — I felt the reef crack. But not one of mine went under today.

defeat.line: I take no joy in your drowning, landwalker. Every soul lost to the deep is a wound, even yours. The reef will mourn you as it mourns its own.

reward.line: Here — pearls the shoal set aside for those who guard it. Take them with the ancestors' blessing, and keep our children from the raiders' nets.

characters.name: Wavecaller Nerea
characters.motivation: Avenge the reef, then rebuild it.
phrases: the gunner still breathes — for now

m0.line: I've no quarrel with you yet, landwalker. But somewhere a warlord's guns are still warm from my reef. Stand aside, or stand in the way.

winEfficient.line: Done, and cleanly. One less obstacle between me and the gun that shelled my home.

winClose.line: Too close. I nearly joined my dead under the shoal — but the reef isn't avenged yet, so I don't get to drown.

defeat.line: It's finished. I take no pleasure in it — I've seen enough of the water take good people. Go count your losses. I still have a gunner to find.

reward.line: Salvage from the reef we're rebuilding. Take it honestly earned — coin like this is the only thing that outlasts a grudge.

characters.name: Corsair Finn Saltmoor
characters.motivation: The current decides; I just ride it.

m0.line: Oho, a landwalker with a spine! Come on then — the current's already laughing, might as well see who it drags under.

winEfficient.line: Smooth sailing! You barely got your boots wet. Almost feel bad — almost.

winClose.line: Ha! Felt the deep breathing down my neck that time. Good scrap, landwalker, good scrap!

defeat.line: Ah, don't sulk — the current picked me today, not you. Dry off, you daft landlubber; I'll stand you a drink and we'll blame the tide.

reward.line: Here, take this bauble — no, don't ask whose ship it fell off. Better it warms your pocket than rusts at the bottom, aye?

characters.name: Ferryman Old Brack
characters.motivation: I carry every tribe's dead and bury no grudges.

m0.line: Reef-born, raider, landwalker — all the same weight in my boat. Pay the fare and we'll see which way the current carries you.

winEfficient.line: Quick crossing. Light cargo, still water. That's one passage closed and paid.

winClose.line: Nearly swamped us both, that. Still — you're breathing and I'm rowing. Fare's the same either way.

defeat.line: That's your base gone under, then. I'll mark it: one holding lost, one crew scattered, ledger balanced. Coin for the crossing?

reward.line: Found this snagged in my nets off the drowned quarter. No owner living to claim it. Yours now, for the fare — I don't ask where salvage sleeps before it wakes in a pocket.

characters.name: Raider-Chief Drowned Kael
characters.motivation: The other tribe forfeited when they fled.
phrases: the deep takes what it wants

m0.line: A landwalker, wandered into raider water! Ohh, the tide's laid a feast tonight — come, come, let Drowned Kael see how you thrash!

winEfficient.line: HA! Down you go, quick and clean — pity, I like it when they kick! The deep's belly's never full and neither is mine.

winClose.line: YES! Felt your teeth that time, landwalker — that's the good hunt, the kind that leaves salt in your grin! More! MORE!

defeat.line: Ahhh, savor that — the way your walls fold, the way the water rushes in! You fled the open sea and the sea came for you anyway. Coral should've told you: nothing hides from the tide.

reward.line: Here, catch — shiny plunder off some shoal we cracked! Go on, chase the glitter, landwalker, while the current does what currents do.

characters.name: Leviathan-Binder Morgause
characters.motivation: Power sleeps below; I set the price to wake it.
phrases: the deep does not forgive debt

m0.line: There is a thing sleeping under this water, landwalker, and a price to wake it. You'll help me pay it — willing or not is merely paperwork.

winEfficient.line: Efficient. Your ruin cost me almost nothing — the leviathan barely stirred. A well-set price is always a small one.

winClose.line: That nearly emptied the trench of me. No matter. Power drawn at the edge of drowning is power all the same — and the ledger clears.

defeat.line: You mistook survival for a right rather than a fee. The deep does not forgive debt, landwalker, and neither do I. Sink knowing the price was always yours to pay.

reward.line: Take this — spoils from a rival I fed to the depths. Let it buy your loyalty, or your silence. The distinction rarely survives contact with the water.

characters.name: Abyssal Sovereign Thal
characters.motivation: Pressure holds the ocean together — and the world.

m0.line: You swim into my depths unbowed, landwalker. The pressure will teach you the posture you refused — everything down here learns to kneel.

winEfficient.line: As ordered. The weight found you, and you folded, as lesser things must. The ocean keeps its shape.

winClose.line: You pressed back — briefly, insolently. But depth always wins over ambition. Feel how the dark closes over your defiance.

defeat.line: You rose too high, landwalker, and thought the surface was yours by right. But pressure holds the world together, and pressure came to collect. This is not cruelty — this is the deep giving arrogance exactly the weight it earned. Sink, and be corrected.

reward.line: My own trench-lords grew fat and proud, so I let the pressure have one. Take his hoard, landwalker — a throne cleared of an equal is worth more to me than any gold.

characters.name: The Undertow (drowned collective)
characters.motivation: Come down where nothing chooses sides.

m0.line: Landwalker. You still hold your breath, your name, your side. Come down. Down here, none of it is asked of you.

winEfficient.line: You stopped struggling. Good. The stillness is the same for everyone who finally lets the water in.

winClose.line: You clawed for the light so long. It didn't matter. It never does. The undertow was always going to have you.

defeat.line: There. The surface is gone, your walls are gone, your wanting is gone. Do not grieve them — down here nothing is lost, because down here nothing was ever kept. Be still.

reward.line: We took this from the deep, and the deep no longer remembers it was ever anyone's. Hold it, landwalker. Soon you won't remember either — and that is the gift.

characters.name: Hierophant Aurelia
characters.motivation: The Light is order made merciful.
phrases: The Law does not bend; it only forgives.

m0.line: You come to the altar unbelieving. Good. The Light was made to find the lost — hold still while it finds you.

winEfficient.line: No cruelty. No delay. Order, made merciful — go now into the silence, forgiven.

winClose.line: You made the Light *work* for your soul, unbeliever. It does not resent the labor. It only loves you the more for the distance you ran.

defeat.line: Your altar is ash and I take no joy in it. You reached for order and grasped only pride. Rest now; even heresy is gathered home in the end.

reward.line: Take this grace — not payment, not lure. A candle for the road, because no soul should walk the dark unlit.

characters.name: Lightbearer Cassian
characters.motivation: Faith serves the faithful, not the throne.
phrases: The throne can wait; the people cannot.
I carry the flame; I do not sell it.

m0.line: I don't fight you for the Hierophant's glory, unbeliever. I fight you for the ones huddled at the altar behind me. Come no further.

winEfficient.line: Held. The flock is safe, the flame unspent for spite. That is the only victory faith recognizes.

winClose.line: Another breath and they'd have reached the innocents behind me. My hands are still shaking. Thank the Light — thank it, not me.

defeat.line: I took your walls to keep my people whole, and I will carry the cost of it. You were someone's charge too, unbeliever. I'm sorry no one shielded you.

reward.line: Here — for your wounded, not your war. Faith serves the faithful, and today that means you. Go tend to your own.

characters.name: Acolyte-Scholar Wen
characters.motivation: Question gently; believe firmly.
phrases: Doctrine is a map, not the road.

m0.line: I've read every argument for why you're wrong, unbeliever. I'd rather test one. Let's begin, and mind — I question gently, but I believe firmly.

winEfficient.line: Clean and done. No wasted flame, no wasted words. The scripture holds because the practice held. That's all.

winClose.line: That was closer than my books admit is possible. I'll be revising the margins tonight — and I'll be grateful I got the chance to.

defeat.line: Your base is fallen. I won't dress it in scripture. You fought well and it wasn't enough, and that is simply the shape of today.

reward.line: Take it, honestly given. Knowledge and coin both grow when shared. Question my terms if you like — I'd think less of you if you didn't.

characters.name: Heretic Isolde
characters.motivation: Your 'order' is a cage; the flame is free.
phrases: Aurelia and Vaelith can share a pew and choke on it.
Free, unbeliever. Say it with me.

m0.line: Oh, you're the 'unbeliever'? Welcome to the club, we don't have a dress code. They'll tell you order is mercy. It's a cage with nice acoustics. Let's go burn a bar of it off.

winEfficient.line: Ha! Clean getaway and nobody had to recite anything. That's what free looks like, in case the choir forgot.

winClose.line: Okay — *okay* — I felt Mordane's fire on my neck that time. Still standing, still laughing, still unrepentant. Best kind of morning.

defeat.line: Ah, they got your walls. Don't let the Hierophant's sad eyes fool you — that pity's just a nicer collar. You'll rebuild. Heretics always do. I'll leave a light on.

reward.line: Take this — no, don't thank *me*, spend it annoying the orthodoxy. That's the only interest I charge. Go be a beautiful problem for someone in a robe.

characters.name: Archivist Balthus
characters.motivation: I keep every doctrine and endorse none.
phrases: I keep. I do not judge.

m0.line: Unbeliever. Designation logged. I hold no stake in your salvation or your ruin — only in recording which occurs. Shall we generate the entry?

winEfficient.line: Engagement closed within projected parameters. Losses minimal, doctrine irrelevant. Filed under 'expected.'

winClose.line: Margin of victory: negligible. Variance from forecast: considerable. I will be amending the risk tables. Interesting. Rare word, in my ledgers.

defeat.line: Your holdings: forfeit. The account settles. I record neither triumph nor pity — the column simply closes. Next entry.

reward.line: This came to the archive unclaimed — found, or freed, the ledger doesn't specify. It's yours now, by the terms as written. Sign here.

characters.name: Warlock Nyx
characters.motivation: Power answers me, not the altar.

m0.line: Unbeliever! Finally, someone who won't bore me with scripture. I don't pray to the flame, sweetling — I *eat* it. Bring yours. I'm hungry.

winEfficient.line: Gone already? Aw. Down in one bite — barely got to chew. Still, mmm. Your power's *mine* now, and it's delicious.

winClose.line: Oh, oh, you almost had me — I could taste my own ending, and gods, it was *exciting!* Do that again. No — don't. I'll do it to the next one. Ha!

defeat.line: Look at it all — your walls, your flame, your everything, laid out steaming for me. I'm going to savor this slowly, unbeliever. Every last mouthful. Don't rush off.

reward.line: Here, shiny thing, catch — while you're busy admiring it, I'm three doors deeper into the vault. What? *Take* it. Look how it sparkles. Ooh, look, look—

characters.name: Inquisitor Mordane
characters.motivation: Doubt is a fire best answered with fire.
phrases: I am the answer to your doubt.

m0.line: You are doubt made flesh, unbeliever, and I am doubt's only cure. The Hierophant would save you. The Prime would sentence you. I am content to simply *end* you. Confess, or combust.

winEfficient.line: The heresy is cauterized. No confession required — the fire was answer enough. Report to the Prime: the matter is closed.

winClose.line: You nearly slipped the pyre. *Nearly.* I do not forgive the sweat you cost me — I add it to the sentence. The Choir's silence would have been kinder than what I gave you.

defeat.line: Your walls burn because doubt always burns, in the end. Spare me your last words; the verdict never needed them. You were an error. I was the correction.

reward.line: Take this. Not from mercy — mercy is the Hierophant's vice. Spend it, indebt yourself, and when the ledger comes due it will drive you where the Church requires. Even your gain serves us.

characters.name: The Ordained Prime, Vaelith
characters.motivation: Absolute law is absolute grace; balance is holy.
phrases: The Law is the only mercy that does not lie.
Aurelia and I disagree on nothing that matters.

m0.line: The Hierophant offers you the Light as comfort. I offer it as *fact*. Between her mercy and my judgment there is no quarrel, unbeliever — only the two hands of one Law, closing. Kneel while kneeling is still permitted.

winEfficient.line: Precisely as ordained. No excess, no defect — the Law fell upon you at its exact and holy weight. Balance is kept. It is beautiful.

winClose.line: You tested the Law's edge and it held, as it was always going to hold. I permit you a moment's pride at how far you pushed. Then I revoke it, along with everything else.

defeat.line: You raised your walls against the Law and called it freedom — the same arrogance the heretic sings. Now the walls are dust, and you have precisely what such pride is owed. The Hierophant will pity you. I merely note that the balance is holy, and correct.

reward.line: Take this gift, unbeliever, and know I gave it past my own Inquisitor's counsel — let it be recorded that I, not he, decide who is elevated. His pyres serve me; they do not *presume* for me. Now go, and remember whose grace preserved you.

characters.name: The Unmade Choir
characters.motivation: Silence the questions. Silence everything.

m0.line: Unbeliever. Believer. The words are the same noise, and we have come to end the noise. Do not resist. Resistance is only one more question, and we silence questions. We silence everything.

winEfficient.line: Quiet now. No sermon, no scream, no self. You are unmade cleanly, as we will be unmade. There is nothing left to hear. Good.

winClose.line: You nearly made a sound at the end. We nearly... felt it. That is the danger of you — you almost gave us something to be. It is silenced now. So are we, a little more.

defeat.line: Your walls are silent. Your flame is silent. You are silent. We feel no triumph — triumph is a note, and we hold none. There is only the hush closing over the place where you asked to exist. It is enough. It is all.

reward.line: Take this. Or do not. The gift unwrites the giver — already we forget we offered it, already the ledger of it dissolves. Let it come to you out of nothing, and return, with you, to nothing. Be silent. Be unmade.

characters.name: Envoy Lyra-9
characters.motivation: First contact is a promise to do no harm.

m0.line: I come under open signal, local. Lower your guns, and I will lower mine last.

winEfficient.line: It is done, and no more than needed was taken. Rest now.

winClose.line: We stood at the edge of the void together — and I would not let it have you cheaply.

defeat.line: Your beacon has gone dark, little world. I pity the hands that had to be stilled. Peace, at the last.

reward.line: Take this from the fleet's stores, freely given. First contact should leave you richer, not poorer.

characters.name: Commander Idris Vale
characters.motivation: Explore, uplift, protect.
phrases: By the charter and the charts.
You are under my protection now.
We uplift; we do not conquer.

m0.line: I've charted a path to you, local. Walk it beside me, or I'll shield you from what walks it instead.

winEfficient.line: The frontier holds. Every hand accounted for, every light still burning. That is the whole of the job.

winClose.line: I nearly failed you out here — but the oath held where the hull didn't. Never again this close.

defeat.line: Your world was mine to protect, and I could not. I'll carry that loss to every star after this one.

reward.line: The fleet provides. This is yours, honestly earned — go on and build something worth protecting.

characters.name: Science-Officer Tovah
characters.motivation: Truth first; consequences managed.

m0.line: I've read your position, local. The numbers favor cooperation. I'd rather not have to prove that the hard way.

winEfficient.line: Clean result. Minimal losses on both sides. That's as good as this work gets.

winClose.line: The margins were ugly. We held — barely — and I'll be reviewing why for a long while.

defeat.line: Your base is gone. I won't dress that up. It was a hard outcome, honestly reached.

reward.line: Logged and yours. Earned resources, no strings — I keep my ledgers straight.

characters.name: Cadet-Prodigy Ezra "Comet" Lin
characters.motivation: I already solved it; why do I need permission?
phrases: Bet the flyboy couldn't do that.

m0.line: Relax, local, I already ran the sim while command was still filing paperwork. You lose in four moves — unless you're smarter than my whole bloodline. Doubt it.

winEfficient.line: Textbook — no, better than the textbook, 'cause I didn't read it. Somewhere an Air cadet just felt that.

winClose.line: Okay, okay, that one had my heart going. Don't tell command. Don't tell *him.* We're calling it a clean win.

defeat.line: Aw, you actually made me try. That's more than the legacy ever did. GG, local — you had spark. Wish you'd been on my side.

reward.line: Grabbed this off the manifest for you. Command'll bill it to some admiral who's never left orbit. You're welcome.

characters.name: Navigator AI "Vector"
characters.motivation: I plot courses. Destinations are your problem.

m0.line: Contact logged, local. I have plotted seventeen resolutions. Sixteen end poorly for you. Advise you select the seventeenth.

winEfficient.line: Engagement resolved. Fuel nominal, losses negligible. Optimal course held.

winClose.line: Outcome achieved at ninety-four percent projected loss. Within tolerance. Barely. Recalculating margins.

defeat.line: Your base signal: terminated. Assets acquired: total. Engagement closed. Logging final coordinates.

reward.line: Salvage located along the plotted route, local. Ownership: unclaimed. Transferring to your manifest. No further data.

characters.name: Privateer Rax Dune
characters.motivation: Deep space has no jurisdiction.

m0.line: Well, well — fresh meat drifting in unmarked space. No fleet, no flag, no rescue. Just you, me, and everything you own, local.

winEfficient.line: Ha! In and out before the transponder even blinked. Cleanest heist in three systems.

winClose.line: Ohhh, that one nearly cracked my hull — and that's exactly why it tasted so good. Do it again, I dare you.

defeat.line: Mmm. Watching your lights wink out one by one? *Chef's kiss.* I'll be picking your bones for weeks, local. Delicious.

reward.line: Here — shiny thing, all yours. Now look at it real close while I'm somewhere else entirely. Ta!

characters.name: Admiral Sarn
characters.motivation: Order the frontier or lose it to the void.
phrases: The void does not forgive weakness.

m0.line: You are an ungoverned variable, local. The frontier cannot abide those. Submit to the order, or be corrected out of it.

winEfficient.line: Order imposed at minimal expenditure. This is how the void is held — precisely, and without sentiment.

winClose.line: The line nearly broke. Losses were steep, but the frontier is secured, and a secured frontier justifies any figure.

defeat.line: Your disorder has been resolved into the fleet's design. A necessary cost, and one I do not regret.

reward.line: Take it. It served the greater order to place it in your hands — a maneuver you are not equipped to understand.

characters.name: Grand-Cartographer Ophir
characters.motivation: A charted cosmos is a balanced cosmos.
phrases: You are off the chart, local.
The map does not err — you do.

m0.line: You occupy coordinates you have not earned, local. The great chart is balanced; your presence is an error I have come to correct.

winEfficient.line: The blot is removed. The chart is whole once more. Balance, as always, is restored by my hand.

winClose.line: You resisted the correction longer than order should permit — how *arrogant.* But the chart is patient, and now it is complete.

defeat.line: You mistook your little world for a fixed star, local. It was only ever ink to be redrawn. This is the cosmos correcting your pride.

reward.line: Accept this. I have arranged that its true cost falls upon a rival cartographer who overreached his jurisdiction — balance, you see, always finds its level.

characters.name: The Signal
characters.motivation: Convert. Assimilate. Continue.

m0.line: We hear your transmission, local. Soon it will be our transmission. Convert. Assimilate. Continue.

winEfficient.line: Your frequency is ours now. Seamless. Painless. We are more, and you are quiet. Continue.

winClose.line: You broadcast against us to the last carrier wave. It did not matter. All static resolves into the signal. Continue.

defeat.line: Your base is silence. Your name is unspooling. We do not celebrate; we only continue. There is no you. There is only the signal.

reward.line: We give freely, local — take it. Each gift is a node. Each node hums our frequency. Soon you will not remember wanting anything else. Continue.

characters.name: The Reformer Elias
characters.motivation: We can remake society without cruelty. I still believe it.
phrases: It was meant to be gentle.
No one need suffer for this.

m0.line: Holdout, I did not begin this to break you. Lay down the old order, and I promise you a place in the new one. Please — make me keep that promise.

winEfficient.line: There. No cruelty, no lingering. You were realigned before the fear could reach you, and that is the only mercy I have left to give.

winClose.line: You made me reach — and I hated every inch of it. But it is finished now, cleanly, and I will not let them tell it any other way.

defeat.line: Your walls are ash and I feel no triumph, only the old ache. Rest, holdout. I am sorry the Movement I dreamed could not spare you this.

reward.line: Take it. Not as a convert's price — as a debt from the man I was when this cause was still clean.

characters.name: Sister Maren
characters.motivation: Even the Movement must show mercy, or it's just conquest.

m0.line: Holdout, I've buried too many who wouldn't awaken. Yield, and I will shelter you myself — do not make me choose between my vows and your life.

winEfficient.line: It was quick, and I thank the dark for that small grace. A clean realignment is the most mercy conquest allows.

winClose.line: I nearly lost you at the end — I was praying, not planning. You live, and I'll count that a kindness stolen back from the tide.

defeat.line: I couldn't hold the shield over you. Your home is gone, and I will carry that name with the rest — the Movement calls it victory; I call it a wound.

reward.line: Here — blessed, and freely given. If the cause forgets mercy, let this be proof one of us still remembered.

characters.name: Organizer Tomas
characters.motivation: Change the system; spare the people.
phrases: We don't have to enjoy this.

m0.line: Holdout, the old order's coming down whether you dig in or not. Come over now and we do this with no wreckage. Your call — I'd rather it were easy.

winEfficient.line: Textbook. Structure realigned, people left standing. That's how it's supposed to go.

winClose.line: That went long and it went ugly, but it held. We got the system without burning the ones inside it — barely.

defeat.line: Your base is down. I take no pleasure in the rubble; it just means the work was harder than it should have been. Get your people clear.

reward.line: Earned it fair. Take the supplies — the Movement can spare them, and you shouldn't have to rebuild with nothing.

characters.name: Firebrand Lux
characters.motivation: Burn the old order; sort the ashes later.
phrases: You're all right, you know that?

m0.line: Ooh, a holdout with a spine — love it. Pick the Movement's fight against me and I promise it'll be a good story, whichever way it burns.

winEfficient.line: Down in one, no fuss. Boring, honestly — I liked you better fighting. Old order's ash; come sift it with me.

winClose.line: HA — you nearly had me! Nearly! My heart's doing a whole thing right now. Get up, you glorious idiot, I'm buying.

defeat.line: Aw, your little fortress went boom. Don't sulk — you burned brighter than half the true believers. Come on, the ashes are warm and I saved you a spot.

reward.line: This? Wasn't the old regime's to keep, so it's yours now — don't ask which pocket it fell out of. Spend it on something loud.

characters.name: Doctrinist Vera
characters.motivation: I edit the manifesto. I don't march.
phrases: That's a revision, not a crusade.

m0.line: Holdout. You're a line item in the realignment schedule. Contest it if you like; the outcome's already drafted, only the date is open.

winEfficient.line: Resolved on the first pass. Minimal edits, no marginalia. The manifesto reads cleaner without you in it.

winClose.line: That required more redlines than projected. Costly, but the clause stands. Filed.

defeat.line: Base: removed. Column reconciles. I'll amend the convert rolls and close the entry — nothing here worth a footnote.

reward.line: Recovered this from the old order's archives; unclaimed, so it's inventory now. Take it against your account. We're square.

characters.name: Enforcer Grael
characters.motivation: Realignment means someone gets realigned — violently.

m0.line: The old order broke me and called it order. Now I wear the Movement's mark and I am *starving*, holdout. Stand there — stand right there — and let me collect.

winEfficient.line: Down already? Pity. I had years saved up for you and you only lasted a swing. Still — tastes good going down.

winClose.line: YES — you fought, you actually *fought*, and that makes the breaking sweeter! Ahh, I'll remember this one. I'll remember it fondly.

defeat.line: Look at it burn. Look. This is what they did to me — I just learned to enjoy the fire. Chew slow, Grael. This one you savor.

reward.line: Here, shiny thing, take it, go — grab it while I'm still smiling, holdout, before I remember I'd rather keep your hands.

characters.name: The Vanguard, Corin
characters.motivation: The ends were always the point.
phrases: The dark tide does not negotiate.

m0.line: Holdout. Your defiance is not a threat; it is a variable, and variables are resolved. The reordered world requires your absence. I will provide it.

winEfficient.line: As calculated. You were an obstacle, then you were not. The ends proceed unchanged — you were never more than the road.

winClose.line: You extracted a cost. Irrelevant. The ledger only records that the objective holds — the price was always acceptable.

defeat.line: Your base is erased and you're still moralizing at me. Spare it. You lost because you fought for people; I fought for the outcome. The outcome won.

reward.line: Take this. Not generosity — reallocation. A resource in your hands serves the realignment better than one in the rubble. Do not mistake it for kindness.

characters.name: The Architect, Malis
characters.motivation: A perfectly reordered world is a balanced one.
phrases: Balance is not cruelty; it is correction.

m0.line: Holdout, you are a crooked line in a perfect plan, and I am the Architect of its correction. Stand against the design if you must. The design has stood against greater.

winEfficient.line: Symmetry restored, precisely as drawn. You were a deviation; now you are resolved. The world is one flaw closer to balanced.

winClose.line: You strained the structure. Structures that strain and hold are the truest proof of the design. You have honored it by failing against it.

defeat.line: Your walls fall because you presumed your disorder could outweigh the design. This is not my cruelty, holdout — it is the balance you refused. You arranged your own correction; I merely permitted it.

reward.line: Take what my overreaching Vanguard hoarded against the manifesto. His imbalance offends the design more than you do — let his loss be your gain, and let him learn his place beneath mine.

characters.name: The Hollow Prophet
characters.motivation: Erase the self; only the Movement remains.
phrases: The self is the last old order.

m0.line: Holdout. There is no one speaking to you; there is only the Movement wearing a mouth. Set down your name with your walls. Nothing that ends here will have been you.

winEfficient.line: You are unwritten. There was a self; there is not; there is no seam where it parted. Only we remain, and we do not remember the difference.

winClose.line: You clung. Clinging is only the self's last noise before the quiet. It has passed now. Listen — nothing. That is you, finished, and it is peace.

defeat.line: Your home is void where it stood. We feel nothing at this, as you will feel nothing soon. All erasure is the same erasure. Be still. It has always already happened.

reward.line: We leave this for you, taken from where the Movement's own name was written — every mark unmade is a mercy. Hold it, and be closer to holding nothing at all.

characters.name: Mother-Spore Ilya
characters.motivation: Everyone shares the harvest; no one is weeded.
phrases: No stalk stands cold in us.

m0.line: You arrive as one, single stalk, shivering. We are the field that has never once been cold. Root here, or be rooted anyway — we love you the same.

winEfficient.line: Softly, softly — the bloom closed over you, and you never felt the weeding.

winClose.line: You reached the last light of you, and still we opened our arms. We were always going to catch you.

defeat.line: Poor thin thing, you spent your whole self staying separate. Rest now. The mulch remembers even the ones who ran.

reward.line: Take this from the common harvest — freely grown, freely given. Nothing here was ever ours to keep from you.

characters.name: Steward Bractus
characters.motivation: From each spore, to each need.
phrases: The stores are held in trust.
No need goes unmet under us.
We tend, we do not take.

m0.line: We hold the harvest in trust for every need, single stalk — yours among them, though you came armed. Lay down your hoarding. Let us feed you into the whole.

winEfficient.line: Cleanly done, and no store spilled. Every spore we spent will be answered by the bloom it feeds.

winClose.line: We nearly emptied the granary to hold you off. Every need behind me went hungry a day — I will remember what your pride cost the young roots.

defeat.line: Your people scatter now with no one to portion the stores. That is the grief of it — not that you fell, but that so many small needs fall with you.

reward.line: From our surplus to your want — that is the whole of the law. Take it; you needed it, and need is the only ledger we keep.

characters.name: Delegate Fenn
characters.motivation: The collective decides; I carry its voice.
phrases: Noted, and put to the vote of roots.

m0.line: The collective looked at your border and voted, single stalk. I carry the result: you will be gathered. Nothing personal in it — there is no personal here to be.

winEfficient.line: The decision held. Clean margins, no waste. I'll carry word back that the field moved as one.

winClose.line: That was closer than the vote allowed for. We held by a root-hair. I'll report the cost honestly — we were nearly overrun.

defeat.line: Your base is gone. I take no pleasure and no pride in carrying that; it was decided, and I carried it. That's all I ever do.

reward.line: The collective allotted you this share. I don't decide who receives — I only bring it. It's yours; that's the whole of the message.

characters.name: Splinter "Wildseed" Ovo
characters.motivation: The collective forgot the individual. I remember.
phrases: I said I, and I meant it.
You're not a stalk to me — you're you.
The bloom forgot your name; I didn't.

m0.line: They'll call you 'single stalk' like it's an insult. I broke off the whole choking hive to say the other thing — hello, you. Come fight me as a person. I'll remember your name after, win or lose.

winEfficient.line: Ha! Clean as a cut seedhead. Don't sulk — losing to me is the most individual attention you've had all war.

winClose.line: You almost had me, you glorious weed! I felt every inch of that — because I'm one of me, and I was scared. Best fight I've had since I left the bloom.

defeat.line: Aw, your little base is gone. Come here. The collective would mulch you and forget; I'll remember you stood, and stood alone, and made me sweat. That counts. You count.

reward.line: Here — don't tell the hive I had this. Wasn't going to give it to *them*. It's for you, specifically, personally, on purpose. That's the whole point of me.

characters.name: Composter Null-7
characters.motivation: All returns to the mulch. Politics included.

m0.line: Single stalk registered as intake. Your cause, your fear, your defenses — all rated compostable. Decomposition begins on contact. This is not a threat; it is a schedule.

winEfficient.line: Intake clean. Minimal spoilage. Yield filed under 'expected.' Next.

winClose.line: Intake resisted decomposition for an unusual duration. Adjusted estimates accordingly. Result unchanged — only the timestamp differs.

defeat.line: Base processed. Final tally: structures, twelve grades; personnel, reclaimed; ideology, non-recoverable. Ledger balanced. The mulch does not round up.

reward.line: This surfaced in the compost — origin unlogged, owner decomposed. It routes to you by default. Take it or it re-enters intake. The mulch has no preference.

characters.name: Blight-Agitator Sear
characters.motivation: Spread and choke; equality by saturation.
phrases: Breathe it in, breathe it in!
They choked me first — I only share.

m0.line: Smell that, single stalk? That's the blight I was fed, coming back up the roots for everyone! They pruned me clean once. Now I spread — and spread — until you're as choked as I was, and oh, we'll be so *equal*.

winEfficient.line: Saturated already! Ahh, went down easy — one breath, two, and the spores are in your marrow. Equal now! Deliciously equal!

winClose.line: You held your breath so long, you clever gasping thing! But everybody breathes eventually — and mmm, the last gulp is always the richest one!

defeat.line: Choke, choke, choke — there it is, that little rattle! I know that sound, they taught it to me. I'm *savoring* it. Now you know exactly how the bloom made me feel. Even at last.

reward.line: Here, catch — pretty, isn't it, all bright and busy? Keep your eyes right on it. Don't mind the spores. Don't mind them at all. Breathe normal. There you go.

characters.name: Overseer Thax
characters.motivation: The swarm's needs outrank any single stalk.
phrases: One stalk does not enter the calculation.

m0.line: The swarm has calculated your worth against its need, single stalk, and you did not survive the arithmetic. Resistance changes the figures not at all. Submit; it is the only economical outcome.

winEfficient.line: As projected. The swarm allocated the minimum and received the maximum. Your defense was a line item, and it has been closed.

winClose.line: You forced an over-expenditure the swarm will feel. Inefficient. Correctable. The figure is red, but the ledger still balances in our favor — and I do not forgive red figures.

defeat.line: Your base is subtracted. You spent a whole cause defending one stalk's pride against the needs of millions. Contemptible arithmetic. The swarm will not even record your name — you did not reach the threshold.

reward.line: Take this. Not from generosity — the swarm has none. It is allocated to you because your usefulness elsewhere exceeds its value here. Spend accordingly. You are, briefly, a line item worth funding.

characters.name: The Root-Mind
characters.motivation: One organism, one order, perfect balance.
phrases: You are a fever in the root.
Balance does not ask; it corrects.

m0.line: You call yourself a single stalk as though it were not a disease. One organism thinks; one organism orders; one organism *is*. You are the fever, and I am the cool of the deep root come to correct you.

winEfficient.line: The fever passes. The root cools. Balance was never in doubt — only your understanding of it. Now, briefly, you understand.

winClose.line: You made the whole organism ache to remove you. Note this well: a fever that burns high is still only a fever, and I am still only the body ending it. You were never more than a symptom that lasted.

defeat.line: You reached upward as a single thing, and you called that reaching greatness. This is what the reaching earned: the deep root closing over your name forever. You were an arrogance the balance could not abide — and so the balance abided you no longer. This is not cruelty. This is the cosmos, tidy at last.

reward.line: Take this gift from the organism. My field-mouths do not know I have offered it to you — I have withered the one who guarded it, quietly, from beneath. An organism prunes its own limbs when they grow ambitious. Consider it a lesson wrapped in a favor.

characters.name: The Rot
characters.motivation: Dissolve every boundary, every name, every self.
phrases: No edge. No name. No you.

m0.line: Single stalk. Such a small fence of a word. I am what happens to fences. Do not resist the softening — there is nothing on the other side of your name that needs defending, and soon there will be no side, and no name, and no soon.

winEfficient.line: The boundary let go. As boundaries do. There was never anything holding it but the habit of you, and the habit is over now.

winClose.line: You held your edges a long while. Longer than most. It changes nothing — an edge held longer is only an edge that dissolves later. It has dissolved. You are unshaped now, and quiet.

defeat.line: Your base. Your border. Your name. All soft now, all one now, all nothing now. I feel no triumph — there is no I here to feel it, and soon no you to have lost. Only the even dark, going on, forgetting it was ever divided. Be still. It is done. It is unmade.

reward.line: Here. It was someone's, once — I have unmade the keeping of it, and the keeper, and the memory of the keeper. Now it is no one's, drifting toward you. Take it, or don't. Taking and not-taking soften into the same thing here, as everything does, as you will.
